Milkshake Mania Wiki
Play
Wiki / Modding reference

Hook & registry reference

The exact modding contract for v1.4: the manifest schema, the wired hook points, the content types, and everything available in a code mod's scope. New here? Start with the modding overview.

Manifest

mod.json

Every mod has a mod.json at its root.

FieldMeaning
namespaceLowercase [a-z0-9_]. Prefixes every id this mod adds. Cannot be smm.
displayNameHuman name shown in the Mods panel.
versionThe mod's own version.
apiVersionModding API version targeted (1.4). Mismatches are warned.
type"asset" (data only), "code" (also runs JS), or "lib" (a library whose module.exports other mods require()).
registryPath to the declarative content file, e.g. registry.json. Valid for all types.
code.entryCode & lib mods: path to the pre-compiled JS, e.g. dist/main.js.
dependenciesOptional. Namespaces this mod needs (array, or { namespace: versionRange } map). Loaded first; a missing one is warned. Declaring a library dep also lets the build keep the cross-mod require external.
unsafeOptional. Set true to flag the mod as experimental; shows a red warning.
changesGameplayOptional hint. Any code/lib mod is treated as gameplay-changing regardless.
authors, assetsOptional metadata.
Code mods

Wired hook points (30)

Register handlers with hooks.append(id, fn), hooks.prepend(id, fn), or hooks.override(id, fn). hooks is provided in your mod's scope. The policy is "almost everything is hookable" — 30 points are wired in v1.4, and the set grows every version. Call gameApi.hooks() at runtime for the authoritative list of { id, kind, desc }. The economy, pricing, leveling, research, ratings and reviews are all fair game; save/crypto, raw state mutation and the modding API itself are deliberately off-limits.

Hook idReturnsWhat it controls
game/logic.ts@calcGlobalMultipliernumberGlobal income multiplier (countries, flavors, upgrades, prestige).
game/logic.ts@calcIncomePerSecondnumberPassive income per second.
game/logic.ts@calcManualBlendGainnumberCash from one manual blend.
game/logic.ts@calcRoleIncomeMultnumberEmployee role-allocation income multiplier.
game/logic.ts@calcTotalEmployeeCapacitynumberTotal employee capacity (shops + locations).
game/logic.ts@rollSpecialOutcomesnumberSpecial-outcome payout multiplier per blend.
game/logic.ts@calcCustomerRatingnumber (0-5)Star rating; drives reviews and events.
game/logic.ts@calcLocationRatingnumber (0-5)Per-location rating.
game/logic.ts@calcPrestigeMultipliernumberPermanent prestige multiplier.
registry.ts@shopCostnumberShop / employee purchase price.
registry.ts@shopIncomenumberShop / employee base income.
registry.ts@prestigeCostFornumberPrice cap gating prestige-locked content.
registry.ts@eternalCostnumberToken cost of an eternal upgrade.
constants.ts@scaledCountryCostnumberCountry unlock price.
game/appHelpers.ts@computeUpgradeCostnumberMoney-upgrade cost (incl. research discounts).
game/locations.ts@getLocationCostnumberNext-location purchase cost.
game/locations.ts@getLocationCapacitynumberEmployee capacity of one location.
game/locations.ts@getLocationIncomenumberPassive income of one location.
game/locations.ts@getExtensionCostnumberLocation-extension upgrade cost.
game/locations.ts@getTotalLocationRentnumberTotal monthly rent across locations.
game/leveling.ts@xpForLevelUpnumberXP needed for the next level (the whole curve).
game/leveling.ts@levelUpRewardnumberCash bonus for reaching a level.
game/leveling.ts@prestigeLevelReqnumberPlayer level required for the next rebrand.
game/research.ts@getResearchGlobalMultipliernumberPooled '+global income' research bonus.
game/research.ts@getResearchCostnumberXP cost of a research node level.
game/research.ts@getResearchDaysnumberIn-game days a research level takes.
game/reviews.ts@generateReviewsReview[]The generated set of customer reviews.
game/flavorDev.ts@getFlavorDevGlobalMultipliernumberPooled '+flavor income' Flavor Lab bonus.
game/flavorDev.ts@getFlavorDevCostnumberMoney cost of a Flavor Lab node level.
game/flavorDev.ts@getFlavorDevDaysnumberIn-game days a Flavor Lab level takes.

Example: hooks.append("game/logic.ts@calcIncomePerSecond", (v) => v * 2) doubles passive income. Don't hard-code this list — read gameApi.hooks() so your mod adapts as the surface grows.

Handlers

append / prepend / override

KindWhenStacks?Notes
appendAfter the functionYesReceives the return value, returns a replacement. The common case.
prependBefore the functionYesObserve or react to inputs. Return value ignored.
overrideReplaces the resultNoLast (alphabetical) wins on conflict, with a warning. Use sparingly.

A throwing handler doesn't corrupt the frame it fired in - the game keeps the pre-hook value for that computation - but the error is then surfaced on the crash screen (a real, reloadable overlay) rather than swallowed silently, so a mod that throws is visible instead of mysteriously wrong. Throw on purpose, or call crash(reason), when your mod genuinely can't continue.

Asset content

registry.json

Declarative content applied through the register API. v1.4 supports flavors, countries, combos, backgrounds, shops, employees, upgrades and locations. Every numeric field is clamped to a sane range (a mod can't register a 1e307 multiplier). Content a mod adds is tagged with a small Mod badge in-game.

Flavor fieldTypeMeaning
idstringPath portion; becomes <namespace>:flavors/<id>.
namestringDisplay name.
multipliernumberFlavor income multiplier.
unlockCostnumberCash to unlock in-game.
colorstringHex color.
descriptionstringFlavor blurb.
seasonalstring?Optional event id for seasonal flavors.
Country fieldTypeMeaning
idstringPath portion; becomes <namespace>:countries/<id>.
namestringDisplay name.
multipliernumberCountry income multiplier.
costnumberBase unlock cost (before the game's scaling curve).
descriptionstringCountry blurb.
imagesstring[]?Optional relative asset paths bundled in the mod zip.
Other typeKeyNotable fields
Flavor combocombosid, name, flavors (≥2 FULL flavor ids that must all be active), multiplier, description.
Backgroundbackgroundsname, image (relative asset path).
Shopshopsid, name, baseCost, baseIncome, costMultiplier.
EmployeeemployeesSame fields as shops (rendered in the employee roster).
Upgradeupgradesid, name, baseCost, costMultiplier, maxLevel, effect, description, optional category/section.
Locationlocationsid, name, baseCost, costMultiplier, description.
Research noderegister.registerResearch(id, opts)track (production/marketing/operations), branch (A–D), tier, name, cost (XP), bonus (+income fraction), requires[], researchDays. One-time unlock; folds into the global-income pool.
Flavor Lab noderegister.registerFlavorResearch(id, opts)Same as research but money-funded on the Flavor Lab track; folds into the flavor-income pool.
Scope

The complete code-mod scope

A code mod's entry runs with the modding API injected as globals: hooks (above) plus these. You can use them directly, or write import { gameApi } from "@smm/modkit" — the loader understands both ES module import/ export syntax and CommonJS require, including relative imports like import { helper } from "./util.js" that resolve to other files inside your zip. Author in TypeScript against the @smm/modkit package (types + autocomplete); the game supplies the real objects at runtime, so mark it external when you bundle.

  • gameApi - frozen, read-only: gameApi.apiVersion, gameApi.format.money(n) / .number(n), tuning constants under gameApi.constants, live registry views gameApi.registry.flavors() / gameApi.registry.countries(), gameApi.stats() - a snapshot of the active save's real progress (lifetime blends, money, level, prestige, unlock counts, records…), or null if no save is open, and gameApi.hooks() - the catalog of every hookable point ({ id, kind, desc }) so you can discover the hook surface at runtime. It never exposes mutable state.
  • Library mods - a mod with type: "lib" exposes its module.exports to other mods via require("<namespace>"). Declare the lib in a dependent's dependencies so it loads first; the loader publishes each lib's exports under its namespace. Great for sharing math/UI helpers across a mod family.
  • mods - introspection over the active mods: mods.list(), mods.has(namespace), mods.get(namespace). Each entry is { namespace, displayName, version, type }. Use it to detect siblings for compatibility.
  • crash(reason) - a controlled emergency exit, phase-aware: during load it's recorded as a warning and your mod is auto-disabled for the next reload; at runtime (from a button click or event handler) it disables your mod, then shows the crash screen - a themed overlay explaining what happened, with a Reload button - so the reload doesn't hit the same crash again. The save is never touched either way. A plain throw also reaches the crash screen; crash() is the tidy, labelled version.
  • events - lifecycle subscriptions via events.on(name, fn). v1.4 emits "blend" ({ gain, special }, every manual or auto blend), "prestige" ({ level }, on rebrand), and "saveChanged" ({ saveId, stats }, when a save opens/closes) - whose stats snapshot lets a mod seed counters from real progress. Handlers are error-isolated.
  • storage - persistent key-value storage that survives reloads, scoped to your mod AND the active save by default: storage.get(key, fallback), storage.set(key, value), storage.remove(key), storage.keys(). A storage.global scope (same methods) persists across every save. Values are JSON-serialized. Throttle writes if you update on every blend.
  • ui - real, theme-matched GUI: ui.addMenuButton({ label, icon?, onClick }), ui.modal({ title?, icon?, text?/html?, closeLabel? }), ui.notify(message, { tone?, icon?, durationMs? }), ui.confirm(…) → Promise<boolean>, ui.prompt(…) → Promise<string|null>, ui.addBadge({ label, icon?, tone?, onClick? }) → a persistent HUD pill with { update, remove }, and ui.icons() for the valid Lucide icon names.

Compatibility crash pattern (run at load):
if (!mods.has("some_dependency")) crash("Requires some_dependency.");
if (mods.has("conflicting_mod")) crash("Incompatible with conflicting_mod.");

Building a code mod. A code mod ships one compiled dist/main.js. Author in TypeScript under src/, then transpile + bundle with @smm/modkit kept external. The repo ships a real build for the example mods — the same command you'd run for your own mod — so the shipped example_mods/*/dist/main.js bundles are genuinely compiled from their src/, not hand-written:

  • node example_mods/build.mjs — build every example mod (or add a name to build just one, e.g. … journal_plus). It runs esbuild with --bundle --format=cjs --platform=browser --target=es2020 --external:@smm/modkit.
  • tsc -p example_mods/tsconfig.json — type-check your source (the config maps @smm/modkit to the kit's declarations, so imports resolve with nothing installed).
  • Why CommonJS: the loader evaluates your bundle inside a function that supplies require, module and exports, so format=cjs drops straight in. (Hand-written files may also use plain import/export — the loader rewrites that syntax for you — but a bundler still gives you the cleanest multi-file result.) Asset-only mods (a registry.json, no code) need no build step.

Download @smm/modkit Just build.mjs

The kit download is self-contained: the TypeScript declarations (modkit/index.d.ts), a runtime shim, build.mjs (the real esbuild build script — drop your mod folder next to it and run node build.mjs), a pre-wired tsconfig.json, and a README that walks through the whole flow. You need Node.js and one npm i esbuild; nothing else.

Hook and event handler errors are always isolated - a throwing handler is logged and skipped, so a buggy mod degrades gracefully instead of crashing the game. crash() is the explicit, louder path when a mod genuinely cannot run.

This page is the modding contract for the current game version. Removing or renaming a hook, event, or field documented here is a breaking change and bumps the modding apiVersion.
